title: Backpropagation Applied to Handwritten Zip Code Recognition
abstract: The ability of learning networks to generalize can be greatly enhanced by providing constraints from the task domain. This paper demonstrates how such constraints can be integrated into a backpropagation network through the architecture of the network. This approach has been successfully applied to the recognition of handwritten zip code digits provided by the U.S. Postal Service.

sources.title: Learning representations by back-propagating errors
sources.abstract: We describe a new learning procedure, back-propagation, for networks of neurone-like units. The procedure repeatedly adjusts the weights of the connections in the network so as to minimize a measure of the difference between the actual output vector of the net and the desired output vector.
sources.relevance: Supplies the training procedure the paper applies under architectural constraints.

sources.title: Neural Network Recognizer for Hand-Written Zip Code Digits
sources.abstract: This paper describes the construction of a system that recognizes hand-printed digits, using a combination of classical techniques and neural-net methods, applied to U.S. Postal Service zip codes.
sources.relevance: The hand-engineered-features baseline on the same task that the paper replaces with learned features.
sources.full_text: Include the plain-text full paper here when available; it enables the read_span and search_sources tools.
